questions.prompt: Solve the equation: (x + 3)/(x - 2) = 5/(x - 2). Which of the following is true?
options: A) x = 2 is the only solution
B) x = 2 is an extraneous solution; there are no valid solutions
C) x = 3 is the only solution
D) x = 2 and x = 3 are both valid solutions
questions.correctAnswer: B) x = 2 is an extraneous solution; there are no valid solutions
questions.explanation: When solving this equation, we first note that x ≠ 2 (restriction from denominators). Multiplying both sides by (x - 2) gives: x + 3 = 5, so x = 2. However, x = 2 violates the restriction established at the start—it makes the denominator zero. Therefore, x = 2 is an extraneous solution and must be rejected. The equation has no valid solutions. This teaches the critical skill of checking that solutions don't violate domain restrictions.
